comparison_checkpoints.certificate_value: SPARE PARTS FOR : DOMESTIC APPLIANCES FLOORCARE CONSUMABLES AND ACCESSORIES, referred to detailed invoice-no 60275954 dated 27.02.2026
comparison_checkpoints.invoice_value: Invoice 60275954, dated 27-02-2026, containing 112 line items of spare parts for domestic appliances (vacuum cleaners, refrigerators, dishwashers, etc.)

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers
comparison_steps.result: No specific article numbers in certificate. Certificate uses generalized description with invoice reference. Proceed to Priority 3.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 – Priority 3: General goods description with invoice reference
comparison_steps.result: Certificate references 'invoice-no 60275954 dated 27.02.2026'. Invoice number is 60275954, dated 27-02-2026. Invoice number matches exactly. Date 27.02.2026 = 27-02-2026 after format normalization → MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that invoice content is consistent with certificate description
comparison_steps.result: Certificate describes 'SPARE PARTS FOR : DOMESTIC APPLIANCES FLOORCARE CONSUMABLES AND ACCESSORIES'. Invoice contains 112 line items of spare parts for domestic appliances including vacuum cleaners (floorcare), refrigerators, dishwashers, etc. The invoice content is fully consistent with the certificate's generalized description.
Certificate contains 'referred to detailed invoice-no 60275954' – this constitutes an express reference to the invoice.

rules_applied.description: General goods description with invoice reference – invoice number and date verified, express reference present, content consistent

comparison_checkpoints.debug_explanation: The certificate uses a generalized goods description 'SPARE PARTS FOR : DOMESTIC APPLIANCES FLOORCARE CONSUMABLES AND ACCESSORIES' with an express reference to 'detailed invoice-no 60275954 dated 27.02.2026'. The invoice number 60275954 matches exactly. The date 27.02.2026 matches 27-02-2026 after date format normalization. The invoice contains spare parts for domestic appliances (vacuum cleaners, refrigerators, dishwashers, washing machines, cooking tops, etc.) which is fully consistent with the certificate's description. Per rule 4.3.5, the goods description is verified.

evidence.raw_text: SPARE PARTS FOR : DOMESTIC APPLIANCES FLOORCARE CONSUMABLES AND ACCESSORIES referred to detailed invoice-no 60275954 dated 27.02.2026

comparison_checkpoints.invoice_value: Net weight 684.327 KGM / Gross weight 825.331 KGM

comparison_steps.description: Determine which quantity in certificate is in box 7
comparison_steps.result: Box 7 contains both 'gross: 986,000 kg' and 'net: 684,327 kg'. Both are verifying quantities.

comparison_steps.description: Compare net weight between certificate and invoice
comparison_steps.result: Certificate net: 684,327 kg. Invoice net weight: 684.327 KGM. The certificate uses comma as thousands separator (European format): 684,327 kg = 684.327 kg. This matches the invoice's 684.327 KGM exactly → MATCH for net weight.

comparison_steps.description: Compare gross weight between certificate and invoice
comparison_steps.result: Certificate gross: 986,000 kg. Invoice gross weight: 825.331 KGM. The certificate value 986,000 kg (= 986.000 kg using European comma-as-thousands-separator notation) differs significantly from invoice's 825.331 kg. The difference is 986.000 - 825.331 = 160.669 kg, which is approximately 19.5%. This far exceeds the ±0.1% tolerance.

comparison_steps.description: Check if both documents state gross weight and values differ
comparison_steps.result: Both certificate and invoice explicitly state gross weight. Certificate: 986.000 kg GW. Invoice: 825.331 kg GW. These are explicit values for the same quantity type (gross weight) and they differ significantly (19.5%). Per rule 4.4.3.1, this constitutes an explicit contradiction → MISMATCH.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 contains both GW and NW – both are verifying quantities
Forekomstkontroll – net weight matches, gross weight does not match
Explicit contradiction – both documents state GW but values differ significantly (986.000 vs 825.331)

normalizations_applied: Thousands separator: certificate uses comma (986,000 = 986.000 kg; 684,327 = 684.327 kg) per European notation

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: 986,000 could mean 986000 kg (nine hundred eighty-six thousand kg)
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. In context, the certificate lists both 'gross: 986,000 kg' and 'net: 684,327 kg'. The net weight 684,327 clearly maps to 684.327 kg (matching the invoice). Therefore the comma is used as decimal separator (or more precisely, the certificate uses period-less notation with comma as thousands separator for the gross value 986.000 and as decimal for net 684.327). However, examining more carefully: 684,327 with comma as thousands separator would be 684327 kg which does not match 684.327. So the comma MUST be a decimal separator in both cases: gross = 986.000 kg and net = 684.327 kg. This interpretation is confirmed by the invoice net weight being exactly 684.327 KGM.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The GW discrepancy might be within tolerance
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. 986.000 vs 825.331 is a difference of 160.669 kg (19.5%), far exceeding ±0.1% tolerance.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Only one quantity needs to match per 4.4.3.1
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. Rule 4.4.3.1 states that if the invoice EXPLICITLY states a gross weight that DIFFERS from the certificate's gross weight, this is an explicit contradiction → MISMATCH, even if net weight matches. 'Att NW matchar räddar INTE en GW-motsägelse.'

comparison_checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 states gross: 986,000 kg and net: 684,327 kg. Using comma as decimal separator (confirmed by net weight matching invoice 684.327 KGM), the certificate values are GW=986.000 kg and NW=684.327 kg. Invoice states Net weight 684.327 KGM (MATCH) and Gross weight 825.331 KGM (MISMATCH). The gross weight discrepancy is 986.000 vs 825.331 = 160.669 kg difference (19.5%). Per rule 4.4.3.1, both documents explicitly state gross weight and the values differ, constituting an explicit contradiction. Per the critical rule 'Att NW matchar räddar INTE en GW-motsägelse', the net weight match does not override the gross weight mismatch. Result: MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Extract all origin countries from invoice line items
comparison_steps.result: Invoice contains per-line Country of Origin: DE (Germany), CN (China), HU (Hungary), TR (TÜRKIYE), IT (Italy), HK (Hong Kong), PL (Poland), SK (Slovak Republic), FR (France), SE (Sweden), SI (Slovenia), TW (Taiwan), GB (Great Britain & N.I.), LU (Luxembourg), RO (Romania).

comparison_steps.description: Verify each certificate origin country can be found in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Germany → DE/Germany ✓; China → CN/China ✓; Hungary → HU/Hungary ✓; Türkiye → TR/TÜRKIYE ✓; Italy → IT/Italy ✓; Hong Kong → HK/Hong Kong ✓; Poland → PL/Poland ✓; Slovakia → SK/Slovak Republic ✓ (normalization: Slovakia = Slovak Republic); France → FR/France ✓; Sweden → SE/Sweden ✓; Slovenia → SI/Slovenia ✓; Taiwan → TW/Taiwan ✓; United Kingdom → GB/Great Britain & N.I. ✓ (normalization: United Kingdom = Great Britain & N.I.); Luxembourg → LU/Luxembourg ✓; Romania → RO/Romania ✓. ALL 15 countries verified.

Certificate lists origins in field 6 at the top and refers to detailed invoice. The invoice contains per-line origin mapping. Certificate does not map individual articles to origins but references the invoice which does. Per 4.3.7, this is acceptable when certificate contains express reference to invoice.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via per-line Country Origin column in invoice
Country name normalization: Slovakia = Slovak Republic; United Kingdom = Great Britain & N.I.; Türkiye = TÜRKIYE (case)
Mixed origins – certificate references invoice for detailed mapping

normalizations_applied: Slovakia ↔ Slovak Republic (standard country name variation)
United Kingdom ↔ Great Britain & N.I. (standard country name variation)
comparison_checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate lists 15 origin countries in field 6. The invoice contains a 'Country Origin' column per line item. Every single origin country listed in the certificate can be explicitly identified in the invoice's per-line origin data. Slovakia is normalized to Slovak Republic, and United Kingdom to Great Britain & N.I. – both are standard country name variations. No extra non-certificate origins appear in the invoice. All 15 countries verified → MATCH.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet och fakturan stämmer överens avseende avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning och ursprungsländer. Däremot anger certifikatet en bruttovikt på 986,000 kg medan fakturan anger bruttovikt 825,331 kg – en avvikelse på cirka 19,5 %. Nettovikten stämmer (684,327 kg). Dokumenten kan inte godkännas automatiskt på grund av bruttoviktavvikelsen.
overall_assessment.summary: 4 of 5 checkpoints MATCH. Checkpoint 4.4 (Quantity) is MISMATCH due to gross weight discrepancy: certificate states 986.000 kg GW while invoice states 825.331 kg GW (19.5% difference). Net weight matches exactly (684.327 kg). This explicit contradiction on gross weight results in NOT_IDENTICAL.

blocking_issues.issue: Gross weight mismatch: certificate 986.000 kg vs invoice 825.331 kg (19.5% difference). Both documents explicitly state gross weight. This is an explicit contradiction per rule 4.4.3.1.

human_readable_report.title: Verification Report – Certificate of Origin vs Invoice

sections.invoice_value: Gross weight 825.331 KGM / Net weight 684.327 KGM
sections.rule_applied: Explicit contradiction on gross weight. Certificate states 986.000 kg GW, invoice states 825.331 kg GW. Net weight matches (684.327 kg). Per rule 4.4.3.1, an explicit GW contradiction is MISMATCH even when NW matches.
sections.motivation: Certificate box 7 states gross: 986,000 kg and net: 684,327 kg. Using comma as decimal separator (confirmed by net weight matching invoice's 684.327 KGM), the certificate gross weight is 986.000 kg. The invoice explicitly states Gross weight 825.331 KGM. Both documents state gross weight explicitly and the values differ by 160.669 kg (19.5%), far exceeding the ±0.1% tolerance. Per rule 4.4.3.1, this is an explicit contradiction for gross weight, resulting in MISMATCH regardless of net weight matching.
